quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier represents a strictly larger percentage decline in global poverty rates. The core scenario—a decline in global poverty—is preserved across all tiers, with only the magnitude of the reduction varying. The gaps between tiers widen at higher levels to reflect increasingly transformative but still plausible magnitudes of poverty reduction.

preference_direction: Greater reductions in global poverty rates mean fewer people living in poverty, which is universally preferable. Tier 7 (a 90% decline) represents the near-elimination of global poverty and is therefore the most preferable outcome.
